title: ICSA-26-190-02 — Schneider PowerChute Serial Shutdown (July 9 Advisory)
summary: CISA advisory for Schneider Electric PowerChute Serial Shutdown UPS management software. Manages uninterruptible power supplies and enables graceful system shutdowns for desktops, servers, and workstations. Deployed on OT servers, EWS, and SCADA hosts.
otRelevance: MEDIUM — PowerChute manages UPS systems on OT servers and workstations. Compromise could enable forced shutdown of critical OT systems.

title: Alibaba Bans Anthropic Claude Code — China Detection Backdoor Alleged (July 10)
summary: Alibaba banned employee use of Anthropic's Claude Code effective July 10 after security researchers found hidden code designed to detect China-based users or Chinese AI lab affiliations. China issued official warning about 'security backdoor.' Employees told to switch to Qoder. Part of escalating US-China AI cold war.
source: Tom's Hardware / CNBC / TechCrunch / CBS News (July 2026)
otRelevance: LOW — Claude Code is a developer tool, not an OT product. But broader Anthropic API restrictions could affect [platform] model availability if this escalates.

title: Digi International Serial Device Server — Critical Authentication Bypass (July 7 Advisory)
summary: CISA ICS advisory for Digi International serial device servers: critical authentication bypass vulnerability. Third serial-to-Ethernet vendor hit this month after Moxa NPort and Lantronix EDS5000. Serial device servers bridge legacy PLCs, RTUs, and instruments to IP networks.
otRelevance: CRITICAL — Third serial-to-Ethernet vendor this month. Concentrated attack surface pattern on OT serial device bridges. Isolate from internet and patch immediately.

title: ICSA-26-188-01 — Hydro-Québec EV Charging Station Backend (Privilege Escalation + DoS)
summary: CISA advisory for Hydro-Québec Le Circuit Electrique charging station backend: privilege escalation and denial-of-service vulnerabilities. EV charging infrastructure is grid-connected OT.
otRelevance: MEDIUM — EV charging infrastructure is grid-connected OT. Compromise could affect electrical distribution load management.

title: CVE-2026-45659 — SharePoint RCE Actively Exploited (KEV Overdue Since July 4)
summary: Microsoft SharePoint Server RCE (CVE-2026-45659) added to CISA KEV July 1 with due date July 4 (overdue). CISA confirms threat actors actively exploiting recently patched vulnerability. SharePoint widely used for engineering document management in OT environments.
otRelevance: MEDIUM-HIGH — SharePoint servers host engineering procedures, P&IDs, safety documentation, and operational records in OT environments. Compromised SharePoint can serve as a pivot point into OT networks.

title: ICSA-26-181-01 — Mitsubishi MELSOFT Update Manager (July 2 Advisory Batch)
summary: CISA released 6 ICS advisories on July 2, 2026. Mitsubishi Electric MELSOFT Update Manager (SW1DND-UDM-M) is one confirmed advisory. MELSOFT is the primary engineering software suite for Mitsubishi PLCs. Remaining 4 advisories pending enumeration.
otRelevance: HIGH — MELSOFT is the engineering software for Mitsubishi MELSEC PLCs deployed in manufacturing, water, and building automation. Compromise of the update manager enables supply-chain-style injection into PLC programming environments.

title: CubeSpace CW0057 Satellite Reaction Wheel Firmware — Malicious Code Injection (CISA July 2)
summary: CISA advisory for CubeSpace CW0057 satellite reaction wheels: firmware vulnerability allows malicious code injection into critical spacecraft systems. Reaction wheels control spacecraft orientation — compromise affects satellite-based SCADA, GPS timing, and remote OT monitoring.
otRelevance: MEDIUM-HIGH — Satellite reaction wheel compromise affects SCADA communications, GPS grid synchronization, and remote OT monitoring. Aligns with Iran's confirmed satellite/space infrastructure targeting.

title: CISA Releases 6 ICS Advisories July 2 — WaterISAC Confirmed (Details Pending)
summary: CISA released 6 Industrial Control Systems advisories on July 2, 2026, for products used across multiple sectors. WaterISAC confirmed the release. Full advisory list pending enrichment on next pull.
otRelevance: HIGH — Multi-sector ICS advisory batch. WaterISAC distribution indicates water/wastewater sector relevance. Full enrichment pending.

title: ICSA-26-181-03 — Schneider EcoStruxure IT Data Center Expert (CVE-2026-8045, June 30 Advisory Batch)
summary: CISA released 8 ICS advisories on June 30, 2026. New: Schneider Electric EcoStruxure IT Data Center Expert (CVE-2026-8045) affecting versions ≤9.1.1 and 9.1.2. Data center infrastructure management platform used in OT-adjacent facilities.
otRelevance: MEDIUM-HIGH — EcoStruxure IT manages data center power, cooling, and environmental monitoring. In OT environments, data centers host SCADA servers, historians, and engineering workstations. Compromise of DCIM platform enables physical infrastructure disruption.

title: CVE-2026-13207 — FUXA SCADA/HMI Authentication Bypass (CISA Advisory, July 15 Webinar)
summary: CISA advisory for FUXA open-source web-based SCADA/HMI platform: authentication bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-13207) exposing user roles. CISA scheduling July 15 webinar for critical infrastructure operators. FUXA deployed in water utilities, energy monitoring, and industrial automation.
otRelevance: HIGH — FUXA is a SCADA/HMI platform used in water, energy, and industrial automation. Authentication bypass enables unauthorized access to process visualization and control interfaces. Isolate from internet and patch.

title: CVE-2026-13207 — FUXA SCADA/HMI Authentication Bypass — Unauthenticated User Role Enumeration (June 30 Advisory)
summary: Frangoteam FUXA web-based SCADA/HMI software contains an authentication bypass (CVE-2026-13207) allowing unauthenticated attackers to remotely enumerate user accounts and view assigned roles — effectively mapping the entire privilege hierarchy of an OT environment.
otRelevance: HIGH — FUXA is an open-source web SCADA/HMI platform. User role enumeration is reconnaissance that enables targeted credential attacks and privilege escalation. If internet-exposed, treat as ACT_NOW.

title: Delta DVP12SE PLCs — Unpatched Modbus TCP Flaws → Remote Attacks (June 30 Advisory)
summary: CISA advisory for Delta Electronics DVP12SE PLCs: unpatched Modbus TCP vulnerabilities allowing remote attacks. Delta DVP series PLCs are widely deployed in manufacturing, building automation, and water/wastewater.
otRelevance: CRITICAL — Unpatched Modbus TCP flaws on PLCs are a direct path to process disruption. Apply compensating controls (network segmentation, Modbus access control) until patch available.

title: StoneFly Storage Concentrator — Root Access + Full Data Control (June 30 Advisory)
summary: CISA advisory for StoneFly Storage Concentrator: vulnerabilities granting root access and full data control. Storage concentrators in OT environments manage historian data, backup archives, and configuration repositories.
otRelevance: MEDIUM-HIGH — Root access on OT storage concentrators means full control of historian data, backup archives, and configuration repositories. Patch immediately.

title: B&R Industrial Terminals — XZ Utils CVE-2025-31115 — CISA Urges Immediate Patching (June 30 Advisory)
summary: CISA advisory for B&R Industrial Terminals (ABB subsidiary): XZ Utils supply chain vulnerability CVE-2025-31115. B&R terminals deployed in industrial HMI, motion control, and automation environments. CISA urging immediate patching.
otRelevance: HIGH — B&R terminals are deployed in industrial HMI, motion control, and automation. XZ Utils is a critical supply chain component. Patch immediately per CISA guidance.

title: Daktronics DMP-5000/8000 Controller Firmware — Unauthenticated Root Access (June 25 Advisory)
summary: CISA advisory for Daktronics DMP-5000, VFC-DMP-5000, and DMP-8000 display controllers: unauthenticated attackers can gain root-level control if devices are left exposed. Controllers manage large-format displays in stadiums, transportation hubs, and industrial facilities.
otRelevance: MEDIUM-HIGH — Daktronics controllers manage critical visual information displays. Root access enables display manipulation and potential network pivoting into connected OT segments.

title: CVE-2026-48558 — SimpleHelp Remote Support Tool (KEV DUE JULY 2)
summary: SimpleHelp remote support tool added to CISA KEV June 29 with due date July 2 (Thursday). SimpleHelp is used for remote OT support and vendor access — compromise of remote support tools is a known OT initial access vector.
otRelevance: MEDIUM — SimpleHelp is used for remote OT vendor support. Compromised remote support tools provide authenticated access to OT environments. Patch before July 2.

title: CISA Daktronics Controller Firmware — Unauthenticated Root Access (DMP-5000/8000, VFC-DMP-5000)
summary: CISA published advisory (June 25) for Daktronics DMP-5000, VFC-DMP-5000, and DMP-8000 display controllers. Vulnerable firmware allows unauthenticated attackers to gain root-level control if devices are left exposed. Daktronics controllers manage digital displays in stadiums, transportation hubs, and industrial facilities.
otRelevance: MEDIUM-HIGH — Daktronics controllers are deployed in critical infrastructure facilities for operational displays, scoreboards, and information systems. Unauthenticated root access on networked display controllers can be used for reconnaissance, lateral movement, or physical safety disruption (display manipulation in control rooms).

title: ICSA-26-176-07 — Schneider PowerLogic P7 Firmware Patch (Multiple CVEs, Reboot Required)
summary: CISA republished Schneider Electric advisory for PowerLogic P7 protection relay firmware. Multiple CVEs including CVE-2026-9718 (authenticated DoS) plus two higher-severity issues requiring a device reboot. Schedule during next maintenance window.
otRelevance: MEDIUM — PowerLogic P7 is a medium-voltage protection relay. Reboot required for higher-severity fixes means a maintenance window is needed. Schedule during next planned outage.

title: ICSA-26-176-03 — Horner Automation Cscape: Local Code Execution + Info Disclosure
summary: CISA advisory for Horner Automation Cscape PLC programming software. Successful exploitation allows a local attacker to disclose information and execute arbitrary code. Cscape is used by engineers to develop and deploy PLC programs.
otRelevance: HIGH — Cscape is PLC programming software used to develop control logic for Horner Automation PLCs (XL, XLe, OCS series) deployed in manufacturing, water, and building automation. Local code execution via an engineering tool targeting developers and OT engineers.

title: CVE-2026-20230 — Cisco UCM SSRF → Unauthenticated File Write → Root (KEV DUE JUNE 28)
summary: Cisco Unified Communications Manager (UCM) and UCM SME contain an SSRF vulnerability allowing an unauthenticated remote attacker to write arbitrary files to the OS, enabling root escalation. Added to CISA KEV June 25. Due date: June 28 (Sunday).
otRelevance: MEDIUM-HIGH — Cisco UCM is deployed for voice communications in enterprise and industrial facilities. SSRF→root escalation on UCM servers can facilitate lateral movement into OT network segments from compromised communication infrastructure.

title: Windows Netlogon RCE Actively Exploited — Critical for OT Engineering Workstations
summary: Critical Windows Netlogon Remote Code Execution vulnerability is now being exploited in active attacks. From the Nightmare Eclipse disclosure series. Engineering Workstations, AD servers, and Windows-based historian servers in OT environments are in scope.
otRelevance: CRITICAL — Domain controllers and EWS in OT networks rely on Netlogon. Active exploitation means unpatched OT-adjacent Windows infrastructure is under immediate threat.

title: ScadaBR CVSS 9.1 Unauthenticated RCE — Missing Auth + Command Injection + Hardcoded Creds (CISA)
summary: CISA advisory for ScadaBR web-native SCADA/HMI platform: combination of missing authentication, command injection, and hard-coded credentials enables unauthenticated remote code execution at CVSS 9.1. ScadaBR deployed in water utilities, energy, and industrial monitoring.
otRelevance: CRITICAL — ScadaBR is a browser-based SCADA platform. CVSS 9.1 unauthenticated RCE on a SCADA system is highest severity. Any internet-facing ScadaBR deployment is ACT_NOW.

title: ICSA-26-174-06 — B&R Linux Kernel Vulnerabilities in Automation Products (Previously Missed)
summary: CISA ICSA-26-174-06 (June 23): B&R (ABB subsidiary) is aware of publicly reported Linux kernel vulnerabilities affecting products including industrial PCs, motion controllers, and automation systems.
otRelevance: HIGH — B&R products run in motion control, robotics, and process automation environments. Linux kernel vulnerabilities in embedded automation devices enable privilege escalation and potential RCE.

title: CVE-2025-67038 — Lantronix EDS5000 Command Injection Actively Exploited (KEV DUE JUNE 26)
summary: Lantronix EDS5000 serial-to-Ethernet device server contains a command injection vulnerability in the HTTP RPC authentication failure logging path. Username is directly concatenated with a shell command without sanitization, enabling unauthenticated RCE. Added to CISA KEV June 23 with due date June 26 (TOMORROW). Actively exploited — Defused Cyber confirmed malware development using this chain.
otRelevance: CRITICAL — Lantronix EDS5000 bridges serial industrial devices (PLCs, RTUs, meters, instruments) to Ethernet. Identical attack surface to Moxa NPort (also recently patched). Root access on EDS5000 = control over all serial devices behind it. Isolate from internet immediately.

title: CVE-2026-34908/34909/34910 — Ubiquiti UniFi OS Max-Severity Flaws (KEV DUE JUNE 26)
summary: Ubiquiti UniFi OS contains three max-severity vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-34908, CVE-2026-34909, CVE-2026-34910) on CISA KEV due June 26 (TOMORROW). Exploited concurrently with Lantronix EDS5000 in active campaigns.
otRelevance: HIGH — Ubiquiti UniFi is widely deployed as network infrastructure in OT environments. Exploitation provides attacker access to network segments and can facilitate lateral movement into OT networks.

title: ICSA-26-162-01 — ABB Freelance DCS Security Lock Flaw → OT Console Hijack (June 11, previously missed)
summary: ABB Freelance distributed control system contains a security lock flaw allowing attackers to hijack OT operator consoles. Coordinated disclosure: ABB and CISA published June 11. Neither has named the reporter. ABB Freelance is deployed in oil/gas, chemical, power, and water/wastewater process control.
otRelevance: CRITICAL — Operator console hijack on a DCS means direct manipulation of process setpoints, alarm overrides, and control commands. This is the operational impact layer, not just a data breach vector.

title: CVE-2026-12569 — PTC Windchill/FlexPLM CVSS 9.3 Unauth RCE CONFIRMED EXPLOITED (KEV Overdue)
summary: PTC Windchill and FlexPLM CVE-2026-12569: CVSS 4.0 score 9.3. Deserialization of untrusted data enables unauthenticated RCE. Affects all releases prior to 11.0 M030. KEV overdue since June 28. Confirmed exploited in the wild.
otRelevance: MEDIUM-HIGH — Windchill manages engineering documentation, BOMs, PLC programs, and safety system documentation for OT assets. RCE on Windchill servers exposes engineering blueprints and design data. Common in manufacturing IT/OT boundary environments.

title: ICSA-26-174-01 — Siemens SINEC INS Critical Command Injection → RCE + DoS
summary: Critical command injection and three additional flaws in Siemens SINEC INS network management platform. Successful exploitation enables remote code execution, denial-of-service, and unauthorized access to sensitive network data. SINEC INS manages Siemens industrial network infrastructure.
otRelevance: HIGH — RCE on SINEC INS provides attacker-level visibility and control over OT network topology. Network management plane compromise is a critical stepping stone to deeper OT access.

title: CVE-2026-1840 — Hubbell Aclara Metrum Unauthenticated Remote OT Device Reboot
summary: Critical flaw in Hubbell Aclara Metrum Cellular Web Interface (firmware < 2.1.0.105) allows unauthenticated attackers to remotely restart OT devices. Deployed in electric and water utility metering infrastructure. Poses severe risk to utility operations.
otRelevance: HIGH — Aclara devices are deployed in electric/water metering and distribution infrastructure. Unauthenticated remote reboot causes loss of monitoring visibility across distribution grids. Update firmware to ≥2.1.0.105 immediately.

title: Siemens SIPROTEC 5 Authenticated File Upload via DIGSI 5 (June 23 Re-release)
summary: Re-released CISA advisory for Siemens SIPROTEC 5 protection relays: authenticated file upload via DIGSI 5 engineering tool could enable malicious firmware or config injection. Requires valid credentials. High severity for electrical grid operations.
otRelevance: HIGH — SIPROTEC 5 protection relays are deployed in electrical substations worldwide. Authenticated file upload via engineering tool is the vector class used in historical relay attacks. Review DIGSI 5 access control and credential hygiene.

title: Dragos Launches EmberAI — OT-Native AI Assistant + 2026 Year in Review Published
summary: Dragos released its 9th annual OT/ICS Cybersecurity Year in Review and simultaneously launched EmberAI, an OT-native AI assistant built on a decade of proprietary OT threat intelligence and incident response data. EmberAI targets the OT security skills gap. Report confirms SYLVANITE→VOLTZITE handoff observed, KAMACITE/ELECTRUM as top disruptors, Volt Typhoon embedded in US utilities.
otRelevance: HIGH — The YIR is the annual benchmark document for OT threat posture. EmberAI is a new OT analyst augmentation tool entering the market. Key YIR confirmation: SYLVANITE→VOLTZITE initial access handoff directly observed by Dragos IR, compressing breach-to-impact timelines.

title: White House EO — AI Cybersecurity Clearinghouse + CWE-306/284/693 Hardening (June 22)
summary: White House EO 'Promoting Advanced Artificial Intelligence Innovation and Security' mandates: voluntary AI cybersecurity clearinghouse (Treasury/NSA/CISA, 30-day formation), CWE-306/284/693 hardening, and NSA-classified AI detection benchmarks. Federal agencies and CMMC contractors face binding timelines; critical infrastructure operators have advisory applicability.
otRelevance: MEDIUM — CWE-306 hardening overlaps directly with active KEV CVE-2026-20253 (Splunk). AI systems deployed in OT SOCs, historian analytics, and IT/OT boundary monitoring are in scope for advisory applicability under this EO.

title: FBI/CISA/NSA/EPA/DOE Joint Advisory — Hackers Targeting Industrial Fluid Monitoring OT Devices
summary: FBI, CISA, NSA, EPA, DOE, and US Cyber Command urgently warn of ongoing cyber exploitation of internet-connected OT devices used to monitor fuel and other industrial liquids. Advisory covers automated fuel/liquid monitoring systems at gas stations, fuel depots, chemical facilities, and related infrastructure.
source: FBI / CISA / NSA / EPA / DOE / US Cyber Command (~June 15-16, 2026)
otRelevance: CRITICAL — Direct OT advisory targeting automated fluid monitoring systems (fuel level gauges, tank monitors, liquid sensing systems). These devices are widely deployed at fuel facilities, water/wastewater, and chemical plants. Internet-facing exposure is the primary attack vector. Disconnect or firewall immediately.

title: ICSA-26-169-07 Schneider Enriched — MiCOM P437/P634 and EPAS-GTW Affected Versions
summary: Specific affected versions now known for Schneider ICSA-26-169-07 (CVE-2026-4827): Easergy MiCOM P437 ≤P437.678.700, MiCOM P634 ≤P634.678.700, EcoStruxure EPAS-GTW ≤6.4.616.200.100. These are protection relays and substation automation gateways in electric sector deployments.
otRelevance: HIGH — MiCOM P437/P634 are protection relays in medium-voltage switchgear and substations. EPAS-GTW is a substation automation gateway. Version specificity enables targeted asset identification.

title: Active Exploitation: Palo Alto + Fortinet Secure Remote Access / VPN (IBM X-Force, June 19)
summary: IBM X-Force reports adversaries running broad exploitation campaigns against Palo Alto Networks and Fortinet secure access solutions. Exploitation grants access to sensitive internal network enclaves. Active in the wild as of June 19, 2026.
otRelevance: CRITICAL — Palo Alto and Fortinet are the dominant IT/OT boundary firewalls in critical infrastructure. Compromise provides direct access to OT segments. Classic pre-positioning pattern for ICS-targeted operations.

title: FortiBleed — 86,644 Fortinet Firewall/VPN Credentials Confirmed (Updated), CVEs: CVE-2026-24858, CVE-2025-59718/59719
summary: FortiBleed count updated: 86,644 confirmed working credentials across 194 countries (previously reported ~74K). CVEs now identified: CVE-2026-24858, CVE-2025-59718, CVE-2025-59719. CISA urging immediate password resets and MFA enforcement. Government and healthcare verticals most heavily exposed.
otRelevance: HIGH — Exposed credentials on OT-boundary Fortinet devices represent a direct path to IT/OT infiltration. Any Fortinet device whose credentials may be in the FortiBleed dataset should be treated as compromised until rotated.

title: CVE-2026-10829 — Moxa NPort Root RCE + Format String Bugs (Patch Available)
summary: Moxa has patched root RCE and format string vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-10829) in NPort device servers. Both bugs live in the device web service. Successful exploitation gives an attacker full root control of the NPort hardware. NPort devices bridge serial industrial equipment to Ethernet networks and are ubiquitous in OT environments.
otRelevance: CRITICAL — Moxa NPort is one of the most widely deployed serial-to-Ethernet converters in OT globally. Root access on NPort means full control of every serial device connected through it: PLCs, RTUs, meters, instruments. Isolate NPort web interfaces immediately and apply patches.

title: CVE-2026-20253 — Splunk Enterprise Missing Auth RCE (KEV OVERDUE + EXPLOITATION CONFIRMED)
summary: Splunk Enterprise CWE-306 vulnerability — Splunk PSIRT confirmed "limited exploitation" in June 2026. CISA KEV due date June 21 (overdue). UNC6508 China-linked + active exploitation. Treat as actively compromised if not yet patched.
otRelevance: HIGH — Splunk Enterprise is widely deployed in OT SOCs, at IT/OT boundaries, and for historian data aggregation and SIEM functions. Unauthenticated file creation/truncation can be used to disable logging, corrupt configuration, or pivot to adjacent systems. UNC6508 China-linked exploitation active.

title: ICSA-26-169-07 — Schneider Electric Easergy/EcoStruxure/PowerLogic Session Prediction (CVE-2026-4827)
summary: High-severity session prediction vulnerability (CVE-2026-4827) in Schneider Electric Easergy protection relays, EcoStruxure, and PowerLogic energy management systems. Attacker who can observe or predict session tokens can hijack authenticated sessions, gaining operator-level access without credentials.
otRelevance: HIGH — Schneider Easergy protection relays are deployed in electrical substations worldwide. Session hijacking enables relay misoperation — potentially causing transformer damage or blackout conditions in the electric sector.

title: ICSA-26-169-01 — Mitsubishi Electric FX5-ENET/IP DoS (All Versions, No Patch Available)
summary: CISA republished Mitsubishi Electric advisory for a denial-of-service vulnerability affecting ALL versions of the FX5-ENET/IP EtherNet/IP communication module. No patch is currently available. Exploitation disrupts fieldbus communications between PLCs and I/O devices.
otRelevance: HIGH — FX5-ENET/IP modules are widely deployed in Mitsubishi MELSEC iQ-F PLC environments. No patch available means compensating controls (network segmentation, ACLs) are the only mitigation path until Mitsubishi releases a fix.

title: ICSA-26-167-02 — Rockwell RSLinx Classic DoS (No Self-Recovery) — Enriched
summary: Rockwell Automation RSLinx Classic becomes unresponsive after exploitation and will not recover on its own, requiring manual restart. RSLinx Classic is the primary EtherNet/IP driver used on engineering workstations, HMI servers, and historians in virtually every Rockwell OT environment.
otRelevance: HIGH — RSLinx Classic is a foundational communication component in Rockwell environments. Combined with ICSA-26-167-03 (PLC MNRF) and ICSA-26-167-05 (FLEX I/O auth bypass), this creates a 3-vector attack surface in Rockwell environments from a single June 16 release.

title: ICSA-26-167-03 — Rockwell CompactLogix 5370/ControlLogix 5570 CIP DoS → Major NonRecoverable Fault
summary: A crafted CIP (Common Industrial Protocol) message can remotely crash Rockwell Automation CompactLogix 5370 and ControlLogix 5570 controllers, causing a Major NonRecoverable Fault (MNRF). MNRF requires manual intervention to recover — the controller cannot self-restart. Successful exploitation results in a complete denial-of-service condition across industrial processes in automotive, food & beverage, and water treatment sectors.
otRelevance: CRITICAL — CompactLogix 5370 and ControlLogix 5570 are among the most widely deployed PLCs in North American manufacturing and critical infrastructure. MNRF causes full uncontrolled process shutdown. Combined with FLEX I/O auth bypass (ICSA-26-167-05), multiple paths to physical disruption exist for attackers with OT network access.

title: ICSA-26-167-05 — Rockwell FLEX I/O EtherNet/IP Adapters: Auth Bypass + Account Takeover
summary: Multiple vulnerabilities in Rockwell Automation FLEX I/O EtherNet/IP Adapters allow unauthorized access, account takeover, and loss of availability. If adapters are reachable from untrusted network segments, an attacker could gain control over I/O data flows — a Stage 2 ICS Kill Chain exposure.
otRelevance: HIGH — FLEX I/O adapters interface directly with physical process inputs and outputs. Account takeover on these devices is a direct path to process manipulation. Verify adapters are not reachable from IT networks or untrusted OT segments.

title: CVE-2026-42897 — Microsoft Exchange Server XSS (CISA KEV, Overdue)
summary: Microsoft Exchange Server cross-site scripting vulnerability actively exploited and added to CISA KEV catalog. Due date May 29, 2026 — now overdue. Relevant for IT/OT boundary environments where Exchange is used for operational communications.
otRelevance: MEDIUM-HIGH — Exchange servers in OT-adjacent IT environments represent a cross-boundary exposure. Compromised Exchange can be used for lateral movement into OT network segments, phishing of OT operators, and interception of operational communications.

title: ICSA-26-148-05 — CP Plus 8Ch NVR Stored XSS (CVE-2026-6824, CVSS 8.4)
summary: CP Plus 8-Channel Network Video Recorder contains a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability (CVE-2026-6824, CVSS 8.4) that allows an authenticated attacker to compromise admin sessions. NVRs are commonly deployed for physical security monitoring in OT environments.
otRelevance: MEDIUM — NVRs in OT environments provide physical security visibility. Compromised NVR admin sessions can blind operators to physical intrusions. OT-adjacent management surface.

title: ICSA-26-148-03 — ABB EIBPORT KNX Gateway (CVE-2021-22291, CVSS 8.0)
summary: ABB EIBPORT KNX building automation gateway contains a high-severity vulnerability (CVE-2021-22291, CVSS 8.0). EIBPORT devices bridge KNX building automation systems to IP networks. Exploitation risk increases if devices are exposed beyond trusted network boundaries.
otRelevance: MEDIUM-HIGH — KNX gateways manage HVAC, lighting, and access control in industrial and commercial facilities. Compromise could allow environmental manipulation affecting OT operations (temperature, access).

title: CVE-2026-33825 BlueHammer — Windows Defender LPE CONFIRMED EXPLOITED (KEV Overdue)
summary: BlueHammer (CVE-2026-33825) Windows Defender LPE is now confirmed exploited in attacks (BleepingComputer June 25). KEV due date was May 6 — overdue. Nightmare Eclipse disclosure series. Allows privilege escalation to SYSTEM on Windows systems. OT workstations and EWS at risk.
otRelevance: HIGH — Engineering Workstations (EWS), operator consoles, and OT-connected IT systems commonly run Windows Defender as the primary EDR/AV. LPE via Defender allows lateral movement from a compromised OT-adjacent workstation to full SYSTEM access, enabling persistence and credential dumping. Common attack path for ICS-targeting threat actors.

title: BREAKING: Unauthorized Access to Anthropic Mythos (Hack-Enabling AI) via Third-Party Vendor
summary: A private Discord group gained unauthorized access to Anthropic Mythos AI model on the same day it was publicly announced. Mythos is described as powerful enough to enable dangerous cyberattacks. Access obtained through a third-party contractor environment by guessing the model URL format based on knowledge of Anthropic URL conventions. Group has been using Mythos regularly since gaining access and provided Bloomberg with screenshots and a live demonstration. Anthropic confirmed investigation: "We are investigating a report claiming unauthorized access to Claude Mythos Preview through one of our third-party vendor environments." No evidence of impact to Anthropic core systems. Mythos was released only to select vendors (Apple, others) under Project Glasswing to prevent bad-actor access.
otRelevance: MODERATE — Mythos is designed to enable offensive cybersecurity research. If weaponized, capability could be directed at OT targets. Supply chain access vector (third-party contractor) is consistent with known OT intrusion patterns. AI-enabled attack tooling represents an emerging threat multiplier for OT environments.
source: Bloomberg / TechCrunch / The Guardian / BBC / Euronews

title: Iran Fires on 3 Ships in Strait of Hormuz — OT Cyber Stand-Down Pledge Under Stress
summary: Iran fired on 3 ships in the Strait of Hormuz (confirmed AP News LIVE, approx 12:39 UTC April 22). Ceasefire nominally extended by Trump but kinetically violated. Iran subsequently signaled delegation has yet to decide on Islamabad talks — coercive dual-track bargaining pattern consistent with prior M/V Touska and Ghalibaf incidents. IC-confirmed IOCONTROL implants remain pre-positioned in US water/power/fuel OT networks. Cyber stand-down pledge mirrors kinetic ceasefire and is under immediate stress. Pre-positioned cells operate on standing orders with no live C2 required. Diplomatic outcome does NOT recall implants. Ceasefire expiry Wednesday evening EDT.
otRelevance: CRITICAL — Pre-positioned IOCONTROL implants in US water/fuel/energy infrastructure remain active regardless of diplomatic outcome. Any kinetic escalation trigger activates standing orders. Sectors: Water, Energy, Fuel, Chemical.
sources: IC reporting (referenced in prior CISA joint advisory)
source: AP News LIVE / IC Joint Advisory (CISA/FBI/NSA/EPA/DOE/CNMF)

title: ICSA-26-111-10: Silex Technology SD-330AC Serial-to-IP Gateway — CVSS 9.8 RCE
summary: CISA advisory ICSA-26-111-10 for Silex Technology SD-330AC serial-to-IP converters. CVSS 9.8 critical RCE vulnerability at OT/IT boundary device category. Affects serial-to-IP gateways used to bridge legacy serial protocols (RS-232/485) to IP networks — common in industrial automation, utility SCADA, and building management systems. No patch available at time of advisory. Mitigations: network isolation, firewall rules.
otRelevance: HIGH — Serial-to-IP gateways sit at the OT/IT boundary. Exploitation allows direct access to serial OT devices without traversing additional network controls. Common in legacy SCADA environments.

title: Self-Spreading npm Supply Chain Attack — Auth Token Theft via Compromised Maintainer Accounts
summary: Active npm supply chain attack (April 22, 2026) using compromised npm maintainer credentials to publish malicious package versions. Attack is self-spreading — malicious packages publish additional malicious packages from compromised accounts. Primary payload: authentication token theft. Separate from and concurrent with March 31 axios RAT attack. Affects any CI/CD pipeline or developer environment running npm install during active window.
otRelevance: MODERATE — Engineering workstations and CI/CD pipelines used for OT software development/deployment at risk. Stolen tokens could provide access to OT application repositories or deployment credentials.

title: Microsoft April 2026 Patch Tuesday — 167 CVEs, 7 Critical RCEs, 2 Zero-Days

CVSS 9.8 actively exploited during confirmed Iranian footholds in U.S. networks. Federal deadline March 26 (BOD 22-01). NERC CIP implications for electric sector.
analystNote: Highest urgency item in current landscape: CVSS 9.8 active exploitation + Iran-US conflict escalation + 7-day federal deadline. This is the exact PLC family in the VOLTZITE intrusion investigation and Framework 3 (Manufacturing) demo scenario.

otRelevance: Three chained vulnerabilities in Rockwell FactoryTalk Optix HMI platform allow unauthenticated remote attackers to achieve persistent access to HMI servers. analystNote: Three-CVE chain: CVE-2024-7591 = initial access (auth bypass), CVE-2024-7592 = privilege escalation, CVE-2024-7593 = persistence (backdoor account creation). Patch immediately. If patching not feasible, isolate all FactoryTalk Optix instances from network access.

title: Siemens SICAM SIAPP SDK — Denial of Service (ICSA-26-076-04)
otRelevance: Attacker can disrupt customer-developed SIAPP or simulation environment. Affects operators who built custom apps on Siemens industrial app platform. Simulation disruption impacts operator training and disaster recovery testing.

title: Schneider Electric EcoStruxure DCE — Hard-Coded Credentials (ICSA-26-076-03)
otRelevance: Hard-coded credentials in EcoStruxure IT Data Center Expert (DCE) — manages power distribution, cooling, and PDUs. Risk is persistent access if SOCKS Proxy feature has ever been enabled by customer.
analystNote: Hard-coded creds are a long-tail risk. Any customer who ever enabled the SOCKS Proxy feature is exposed. Requires admin credentials + proxy feature enabled. Off-by-default reduces immediate blast radius.

title: ABB Ability Symphony Plus DCS — Improper Input Validation, Denial of Service Risk
otRelevance: Symphony Plus DCS widely deployed in power generation, water treatment, and pulp/paper. Improper input validation enables denial-of-service against the DCS server, potentially causing loss of view/control for operators. Operational impact depends on whether redundant DCS controllers are configured.
analystNote: DoS on DCS can result in loss of view and loss of control — high OT impact even without data compromise. Verify redundant controller failover configuration before deprioritizing. CVE-2024-6102 requires NVD verification.

title: Honeywell Experion PKS Historian — SQL Injection, Data Exfiltration Risk
otRelevance: SQL injection in Honeywell Experion PKS historian enables exfiltration of process data — setpoints, historian trends, alarm configurations. KAMACITE has demonstrated specific interest in historian data for OT network mapping. Historian access often bridges IT/OT network segments.
analystNote: Historian SQL injection is particularly valuable to pre-positioned adversaries mapping process behavior for targeted attack development. KAMACITE TTPs specifically include historian exploitation. CVEs require NVD verification.

otRelevance: Vulnerability in Honeywell BMS controlling HVAC, access control, and power management in critical infrastructure facilities including data centers and industrial buildings.

otRelevance: Path traversal in GX Works3 engineering software for MELSEC iQ-R series PLCs. Attacker with access to engineering network can read/write arbitrary files on engineering workstation, enabling exfiltration of PLC programs and potential project file manipulation.
analystNote: Engineering workstations are high-value targets — they hold PLC programs, network topology, and process configuration. Path traversal enabling project file access is a stepping stone to logic modification. CVE-2024-4483 requires NVD verification.

otRelevance: Attempted unauthorized access to internet-facing water treatment HMI via Tor exit node. 4-minute authenticated session (default credentials). Southwest U.S. utilities most affected.
analystNote: See active investigation INV-003. Even without process manipulation, authenticated access to water treatment HMI represents a successful initial access event requiring immediate remediation. Remove default credentials and implement IP allowlisting on all internet-facing HMIs.
